verses.latin_vulgate: Nolite judicare, ut non judicemini.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ κρίνετε ἵνα μὴ κριθῆτε
verses.king_james: Judge not, that ye be not judged.

verses.latin_vulgate: In quo enim judicio judicaveritis, judicabimini: et in qua mensura mensi fueritis, remetietur vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν ᾧ γὰρ κρίματι κρίνετε κριθήσεσθε καὶ ἐν ᾧ μέτρῳ μετρεῖτε ἀντιμετρηθήσεται ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: For with what judgment ye judge, ye shall be judged: and with what measure ye mete, it shall be measured to you again.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid autem vides festucam in oculo fratris tui, et trabem in oculo tuo non vides?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τί δὲ βλέπεις τὸ κάρφος τὸ ἐν τῷ ὀφθαλμῷ τοῦ ἀδελφοῦ σου τὴν δὲ ἐν τῷ σῷ ὀφθαλμῷ δοκὸν οὐ κατανοεῖς
verses.king_james: And why beholdest thou the mote that is in thy brother's eye, but considerest not the beam that is in thine own eye?

verses.latin_vulgate: aut quomodo dicis fratri tuo: Sine ejiciam festucam de oculo tuo, et ecce trabs est in oculo tuo?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἢ πῶς ἐρεῖς τῷ ἀδελφῷ σου ἄφες ἐκβάλω τὸ κάρφος ἀπὸ τοῦ ὀφθαλμοῦ σου καὶ ἰδού ἡ δοκὸς ἐν τῷ ὀφθαλμῷ σοῦ
verses.king_james: Or how wilt thou say to thy brother, Let me pull out the mote out of thine eye; and, behold, a beam *is* in thine own eye?

verses.latin_vulgate: Hypocrita, ejice primum trabem de oculo tuo, et tunc videbis ejicere festucam de oculo fratris tui.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑποκριτά ἔκβαλε πρῶτον τὴν δοκὸν ἐκ τοῦ ὀφθαλμοῦ σοῦ καὶ τότε διαβλέψεις ἐκβαλεῖν τὸ κάρφος ἐκ τοῦ ὀφθαλμοῦ τοῦ ἀδελφοῦ σου
verses.king_james: Thou hypocrite, first cast out the beam out of thine own eye; and then shalt thou see clearly to cast out the mote out of thy brother's eye.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nolite dare sanctum canibus: neque mittatis margaritas vestras ante porcos, ne forte conculcent eas pedibus suis, et conversi dirumpant vos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ δῶτε τὸ ἅγιον τοῖς κυσίν μηδὲ βάλητε τοὺς μαργαρίτας ὑμῶν ἔμπροσθεν τῶν χοίρων μήποτε καταπατήσωσιν αὐτοὺς ἐν τοῖς ποσὶν αὐτῶν καὶ στραφέντες ῥήξωσιν ὑμᾶς
verses.king_james: Give not that which is holy unto the dogs, neither cast ye your pearls before swine, lest they trample them under their feet, and turn again and rend you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Petite, et dabitur vobis: quærite, et invenietis: pulsate, et aperietur vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: αἰτεῖτε καὶ δοθήσεται ὑμῖν ζητεῖτε καὶ εὑρήσετε κρούετε καὶ ἀνοιγήσεται ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: Ask, and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened unto you:

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis enim qui petit, accipit: et qui quærit, invenit: et pulsanti aperietur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πᾶς γὰρ ὁ αἰτῶν λαμβάνει καὶ ὁ ζητῶν εὑρίσκει καὶ τῷ κρούοντι ἀνοιγήσεται
verses.king_james: For every one that asketh receiveth; and he that seeketh findeth; and to him that knocketh it shall be opened.

verses.latin_vulgate: Aut quis est ex vobis homo, quem si petierit filius suus panem, numquid lapidem porriget ei?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἢ τίς ἐστιν ἐξ ὑμῶν ἄνθρωπος ὃν ἐὰν αἰτήσῃ ὁ υἱὸς αὐτοῦ ἄρτον μὴ λίθον ἐπιδώσει αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: Or what man is there of you, whom if his son ask bread, will he give him a stone?

verses.latin_vulgate: aut si piscem petierit, numquid serpentem porriget ei?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐὰν ἰχθὺν αἰτήσῃ μὴ ὄφιν ἐπιδώσει αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: Or if he ask a fish, will he give him a serpent?

verses.latin_vulgate: Si ergo vos, cum sitis mali, nostis bona data dare filiis vestris: quanto magis Pater vester, qui in cælis est, dabit bona petentibus se?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ οὖν ὑμεῖς πονηροὶ ὄντες οἴδατε δόματα ἀγαθὰ διδόναι τοῖς τέκνοις ὑμῶν πόσῳ μᾶλλον ὁ πατὴρ ὑμῶν ὁ ἐν τοῖς οὐρανοῖς δώσει ἀγαθὰ τοῖς αἰτοῦσιν αὐτόν
verses.king_james: If ye then, being evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children, how much more shall your Father which is in heaven give good things to them that ask him?

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnia ergo quæcumque vultis ut faciant vobis homines, et vos facite illis. Hæc est enim lex, et prophetæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάντα οὖν ὅσα ἂν θέλητε ἵνα ποιῶσιν ὑμῖν οἱ ἄνθρωποι οὕτως καὶ ὑμεῖς ποιεῖτε αὐτοῖς οὗτος γάρ ἐστιν ὁ νόμος καὶ οἱ προφῆται
verses.king_james: Therefore all things whatsoever ye would that men should do to you, do ye even so to them: for this is the law and the prophets.

verses.latin_vulgate: Intrate per angustam portam: quia lata porta, et spatiosa via est, quæ ducit ad perditionem, et multi sunt qui intrant per eam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰσέλθετε διὰ τῆς στενῆς πύλης ὅτι πλατεῖα ἡ πύλη καὶ εὐρύχωρος ἡ ὁδὸς ἡ ἀπάγουσα εἰς τὴν ἀπώλειαν καὶ πολλοί εἰσιν οἱ εἰσερχόμενοι δι᾽ αὐτῆς
verses.king_james: Enter ye in at the strait gate: for wide *is* the gate, and broad *is* the way, that leadeth to destruction, and many there be which go in thereat:

verses.latin_vulgate: Quam angusta porta, et arcta via est, quæ ducit ad vitam: et pauci sunt qui inveniunt eam!
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτί στενὴ ἡ πύλη καὶ τεθλιμμένη ἡ ὁδὸς ἡ ἀπάγουσα εἰς τὴν ζωήν καὶ ὀλίγοι εἰσὶν οἱ εὑρίσκοντες αὐτήν
verses.king_james: Because strait *is* the gate, and narrow *is* the way, which leadeth unto life, and few there be that find it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Attendite a falsis prophetis, qui veniunt ad vos in vestimentis ovium, intrinsecus autem sunt lupi rapaces:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προσέχετε δέ ἀπὸ τῶν ψευδοπροφητῶν οἵτινες ἔρχονται πρὸς ὑμᾶς ἐν ἐνδύμασιν προβάτων ἔσωθεν δὲ εἰσιν λύκοι ἅρπαγες
verses.king_james: Beware of false prophets, which come to you in sheep's clothing, but inwardly they are ravening wolves.

verses.latin_vulgate: a fructibus eorum cognoscetis eos. Numquid colligunt de spinas uvas, aut de tribulis ficus?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπὸ τῶν καρπῶν αὐτῶν ἐπιγνώσεσθε αὐτούς μήτι συλλέγουσιν ἀπὸ ἀκανθῶν σταφυλὴν ἢ ἀπὸ τριβόλων σῦκα
verses.king_james: Ye shall know them by their fruits. Do men gather grapes of thorns, or figs of thistles?

verses.latin_vulgate: Sic omnis arbor bona fructus bonos facit: mala autem arbor malos fructus facit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὕτως πᾶν δένδρον ἀγαθὸν καρποὺς καλοὺς ποιεῖ τὸ δὲ σαπρὸν δένδρον καρποὺς πονηροὺς ποιεῖ
verses.king_james: Even so every good tree bringeth forth good fruit; but a corrupt tree bringeth forth evil fruit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non potest arbor bona malos fructus facere: neque arbor mala bonos fructus facere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ δύναται δένδρον ἀγαθὸν καρποὺς πονηροὺς ποιεῖν οὐδὲ δένδρον σαπρὸν καρποὺς καλοὺς ποιεῖν
verses.king_james: A good tree cannot bring forth evil fruit, neither *can* a corrupt tree bring forth good fruit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis arbor, quæ non facit fructum bonum, excidetur, et in ignem mittetur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πᾶν δένδρον μὴ ποιοῦν καρπὸν καλὸν ἐκκόπτεται καὶ εἰς πῦρ βάλλεται
verses.king_james: Every tree that bringeth not forth good fruit is hewn down, and cast into the fire.

verses.latin_vulgate: Igitur ex fructibus eorum cognoscetis eos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄραγε ἀπὸ τῶν καρπῶν αὐτῶν ἐπιγνώσεσθε αὐτούς
verses.king_james: Wherefore by their fruits ye shall know them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non omnis qui dicit mihi, Domine, Domine, intrabit in regnum cælorum: sed qui facit voluntatem Patris mei, qui in cælis est, ipse intrabit in regnum cælorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ πᾶς ὁ λέγων μοι κύριε κύριε εἰσελεύσεται εἰς τὴν βασιλείαν τῶν οὐρανῶν ἀλλ᾽ ὁ ποιῶν τὸ θέλημα τοῦ πατρός μου τοῦ ἐν οὐρανοῖς
verses.king_james: Not every one that saith unto me, Lord, Lord, shall enter into the kingdom of heaven; but he that doeth the will of my Father which is in heaven.

verses.latin_vulgate: Multi dicent mihi in illa die: Domine, Domine, nonne in nomine tuo prophetavimus, et in nomine tuo dæmonia ejecimus, et in nomine tuo virtutes multas fecimus?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πολλοὶ ἐροῦσίν μοι ἐν ἐκείνῃ τῇ ἡμέρᾳ κύριε κύριε οὐ τῷ σῷ ὀνόματι προεφητεύσαμεν καὶ τῷ σῷ ὀνόματι δαιμόνια ἐξεβάλομεν καὶ τῷ σῷ ὀνόματι δυνάμεις πολλὰς ἐποιήσαμεν
verses.king_james: Many will say to me in that day, Lord, Lord, have we not prophesied in thy name? and in thy name have cast out devils? and in thy name done many wonderful works?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et tunc confitebor illis: Quia numquam novi vos: discedite a me, qui operamini iniquitatem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ τότε ὁμολογήσω αὐτοῖς ὅτι οὐδέποτε ἔγνων ὑμᾶς ἀποχωρεῖτε ἀπ᾽ ἐμοῦ οἱ ἐργαζόμενοι τὴν ἀνομίαν
verses.king_james: And then will I profess unto them, I never knew you: depart from me, ye that work iniquity.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis ergo qui audit verba mea hæc, et facit ea, assimilabitur viro sapienti, qui ædificavit domum suam supra petram,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πᾶς οὖν ὅστις ἀκούει μου τοὺς λόγους τούτους καὶ ποιεῖ αὐτοὺς ὁμοιώσω αὐτὸν ἀνδρὶ φρονίμῳ ὅστις ᾠκοδόμησεν τὴν οἰκίαν αὐτοῦ ἐπὶ τὴν πέτραν
verses.king_james: Therefore whosoever heareth these sayings of mine, and doeth them, I will liken him unto a wise man, which built his house upon a rock:

verses.latin_vulgate: et descendit pluvia, et venerunt flumina, et flaverunt venti, et irruerunt in domum illam, et non cecidit: fundata enim erat super petram.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ κατέβη ἡ βροχὴ καὶ ἦλθον οἱ ποταμοὶ καὶ ἔπνευσαν οἱ ἄνεμοι καὶ προσέπεσον τῇ οἰκίᾳ ἐκείνῃ καὶ οὐκ ἔπεσεν τεθεμελίωτο γὰρ ἐπὶ τὴν πέτραν
verses.king_james: And the rain descended, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat upon that house; and it fell not: for it was founded upon a rock.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et omnis qui audit verba mea hæc, et non facit ea, similis erit viro stulto, qui ædificavit domum suam super arenam:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πᾶς ὁ ἀκούων μου τοὺς λόγους τούτους καὶ μὴ ποιῶν αὐτοὺς ὁμοιωθήσεται ἀνδρὶ μωρῷ ὅστις ᾠκοδόμησεν τὴν οἰκίαν αὐτοῦ ἐπὶ τὴν ἄμμον
verses.king_james: And every one that heareth these sayings of mine, and doeth them not, shall be likened unto a foolish man, which built his house upon the sand:

verses.latin_vulgate: et descendit pluvia, et venerunt flumina, et flaverunt venti, et irruerunt in domum illam, et cecidit, et fuit ruina illius magna.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ κατέβη ἡ βροχὴ καὶ ἦλθον οἱ ποταμοὶ καὶ ἔπνευσαν οἱ ἄνεμοι καὶ προσέκοψαν τῇ οἰκίᾳ ἐκείνῃ καὶ ἔπεσεν καὶ ἦν ἡ πτῶσις αὐτῆς μεγάλη
verses.king_james: And the rain descended, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat upon that house; and it fell: and great was the fall of it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est: cum consummasset Jesus verba hæc, admirabantur turbæ super doctrina ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγένετο ὅτε συνετέλεσεν ὁ ἰησοῦς τοὺς λόγους τούτους ἐξεπλήσσοντο οἱ ὄχλοι ἐπὶ τῇ διδαχῇ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, when Jesus had ended these sayings, the people were astonished at his doctrine:

verses.latin_vulgate: Erat enim docens eos sicut potestatem habens, et non sicut scribæ eorum, et pharisæi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν γὰρ διδάσκων αὐτοὺς ὡς ἐξουσίαν ἔχων καὶ οὐχ ὡς οἱ γραμματεῖς
verses.king_james: For he taught them as *one* having authority, and not as the scribes.
